title: Drynaria nectarifera
taxonomy: Drynaria nectarifera is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of climbing or scrambling epiphyte native to tropical regions of Asia. The genus Drynaria is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a large number of fern species. Polypodiaceae is a family of vascular plants commonly known as the polypody ferns. Drynaria nectarifera is a unique species within this family due to its distinctive features.

It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. intro: Drynaria nectarifera, also known as Chinese foxnut or Chinese talus, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. The health benefits of Drynaria nectarifera include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include polysaccharides, flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Drynaria nectarifera include capsules, tablets, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including rheumatism, fever, and digestive disorders.
